key_themes: Q1 FY2026 earnings beat with raised full-year guidance
Recent Citi upgrade to Buy with $33 price target
Persistent debt/leverage concerns and auto industry cyclicality
summary: Adient delivered a solid Q1 2026 beat (Adj. EPS $0.35 vs $0.19 expected; revenue $3.64B vs $3.45B) with year-over-year Adj. EBITDA margin expansion, raised FY2026 revenue/EBITDA/FCF guidance on improved production and onshoring momentum, launched new products like StepJoy, and received a Citi upgrade to Buy ($33 PT) citing expected Q2 beat.[[1]](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/8798942/adient-adnt-upgraded-to-buy-amid-expected-q2-earnings-beat)[[2]](https://www.marketbeat.com/stocks/NYSE/ADNT/earnings/)[[3]](https://investors.adient.com/news-releases/2026/02-04-2026-115012049) Analyst targets average in the high $20s–low $30s (notable upside from ~$22 share price) with forward P/E attractive, though TTM GAAP results remain negative. The biggest risk is high debt (D/E >126%), balance-sheet leverage in a cyclical auto sector vulnerable to production volatility or disruptions.[[4]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/ADNT/)[[5]](https://www.moomoo.com/news/post/31846767/is-adient-nyse-adnt-using-too-much-debt)

recent_posts.highlight: Adient upgraded to BUY at Citi with $33 target; expects Q2 earnings beat and notes 15% underperformance vs S&P since Middle East conflict
Adient upgraded to Buy from Neutral by Citigroup, raises price target to $33 from $30
Deutsche Bank lowers Adient target to $30 from $33 (still Hold rating)
Q2 fiscal 2026 earnings call scheduled for May 6; follows recent guidance raise and operational resilience comments
